---
title: "nitrix"
subtitle: "GPU-accelerated pure numerics for neuroimaging in JAX/XLA"
toc: false
---

`nitrix` is the substrate numerics layer of the federation: statistics,
geometry, registration, signal, graph, and morphology, written as pure
JAX/XLA so they run — differentiably, batched, JIT-compiled — on a hardware
accelerator.

::: {.callout-tldr title="Fast path"}
If you read one chapter, read
[Fellner–Schall: the engine of the statistical suite](explanation/fellner-schall.qmd).
It explains why one small fixed-point update unifies smoothing, mixed models,
and (soon) Gaussian processes across the whole `nitrix.stats` package.
:::

## Explanation (the textbook layer)

- [Fellner–Schall: the engine of the statistical suite](explanation/fellner-schall.qmd)

More chapters (geometry, registration, the semiring substrate) follow the same
pattern: derive the mathematics, justify the design, point at the code.
